title: SpinOUT™: Buffer Exchange
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2jvqn","text":"SpinOUT™ protocol for buffer exchange of peptide and protein samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dct06","text":"(Cat. # 786-170 to 786-173, 786-703 to 786-708, 786-865 to 786-869)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"711n9","text":"INTRODUCTION","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8haf","text":"The SpinOUT™ columns are versatile, spin-format columns for the desalting and buffer exchange of peptide and protein and other macromolecule solutions ranging from 5µl through to 3ml sample volumes. The SpinOUT™ columns are available in three MWCO sizes for \u003e700, \u003e6,000 or \u003e30,000 dalton peptides or proteins and are suitable for samples containing as little as 20µg peptide or protein/ml. The","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2hpif","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2m85n","text":"SpinOUT™ columns are simple to use as the peptide or protein solution is applied and then centrifuged to recover protein with the column retaining \u003e95% of the salts and small molecules (\u003c100Da for SpinOUT™ GT-100, \u003c1,000Da for Spinout™ GT-600 and \u003c1,500 for Spinout™ GT-1200).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dg6mf","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cpbg0","text":"ITEMS SUPPLIED","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"26n4s","text":"   Cat. # steps: Mark one side of the column and ensure in all centrifugations the mark is facing outwards during centrifugation.
Prepare the SpinOUT™ column by centrifuging the SpinOUT™ columns at 1,000g for 1 minute to compact the resin. 00:01:00
Remove the top and then bottom caps. Place into an appropriate collection tube.
Centrifuge the column at 1,000g for 2 minutes to remove the storage buffer. 00:02:00
Place the column in a new collection tube and remove the cap.

Add the buffer to be exchanged into to the column a. For 0.1ml column, use 75µl buffer b. For 1ml column, use 0.5ml buffer c. For 3ml column, use 1.5ml buffer d. For 5ml column, use 2.5ml buffer e. For 10ml column, use 5ml buffer
Centrifuge the column at 1,000g for 2 minutes to remove the buffer. 00:02:00
Repeat steps 6 and 7, a further five more times, ensuring the buffer is discarded after each centrifugation.
Place the column in a new collection tube and remove the cap.

Slowly, apply the protein solution to the center of the SpinOUT™ resin. The recommended volumes to apply to the column are as follows: %s %s Cat. # Description # Supplied Resin Bed Volume (ml) Sample Load Volume (ml) 786-865 SpinOUT™ GT-100, 0.1ml 25 0.1 0.005-0.03 786-866 SpinOUT™ GT-100, 1ml 10 1 0.03-0.3 786-867 SpinOUT™ GT-100, 3ml 10 3 0.3-0.9 786-868 SpinOUT™ GT-100, 5ml 5 5 0.75-1.5 786-869 SpinOUT™ GT-100, 10ml 5 10 1-3 786-703 SpinOUT™ GT-600, 0.1ml 25 0.1 0.005-0.03 786-170 SpinOUT™ GT-600, 1ml 10 1 0.03-0.3 786-171 SpinOUT™ GT-600, 3ml 10 3 0.3-0.9 786-704 SpinOUT™ GT-600, 5ml 5 5 0.75-1.5 786-705 SpinOUT™ GT-600, 10ml 5 10 1-3 786-706 SpinOUT™ GT-1200, 0.1ml 25 0.1 0.005-0.03 786-172 SpinOUT™ GT-1200, 1ml 10 1 0.03-0.3 786-173 SpinOUT™ GT-1200, 3ml 10 3 0.3-0.9 786-707 SpinOUT™ GT-1200, 5ml 5 5 0.75-1.5 786-708 SpinOUT™ GT-1200, 10ml 5 10 1-3 Sample Load Volume The recommended load volumes (see table) are a guideline. The actual volumes used will be dependent on your sample, the concentration of salts and contaminants to beremoved and the recovered purity desired. For optimal removal of contaminants, we recommend using a sample volume of <20% of the resin bed volume. NOTE : Loading more than the recommended load volume will result in a higher level of contaminating salts and other molecules.

Centrifuge the column at 1,000g for the indicated times in the table below to collect the protein solution. Discard the column. %s %s Column size Centrifugation Time (mins) 0.1ml column 4 1ml column 4 3ml column 6 5ml column 8 10ml column 10
creator: Colin Heath
